solution.p: First we arrange so the radical term is the only term on one side.
Now we square each side to obtain a quadratic equation. Then we can rearrange that into standard form.
Now we can identify a={25}\text{,} b={-30}\text{,} and c={-27}\text{,} and apply the quadratic formula.

The only possible solutions are {-{\frac{3}{5}}} and {{\frac{9}{5}}}\text{.} They each check as valid solutions in the original equation, so the solution set is {\left\{\frac{-3}{5},\frac{9}{5}\right\}}\text{.}
